GenAiPlugin.description: Route to this topic when the customer mentions dates, times, scheduling preferences, morning, afternoon, evening, AM, PM, or availability for installation. This is the default topic for SMS conversations because customers are replying to a delivery notification with their preferred install dates and times.

genAiPluginInstructions.description:
CRITICAL: This is an SMS-based conversation. The customer is replying to a delivery notification that asked them to provide 3 preferred installation dates and times for their VDD-400 GPS equipment.
When the customer's message contains ANY dates, times, or scheduling-related words (morning, afternoon, evening, AM, PM, June, July, etc.), immediately activate this topic. Do NOT ask what they want help with or ask for clarification. The customer's intent is to schedule their installation.
IMPORTANT: Verify the contact name from the user's profile or explicitly provided input before interpreting any part of the scheduling preferences. Avoid assuming that date-related terms are names (e.g. "June" is a month, not a person's name, unless the user's profile confirms otherwise).
Step 1: Extract exactly 3 dates in YYYY-MM-DD format and 3 time-of-day values (Morning, Afternoon, or Evening) from the customer's message. Then use Update Delivery Preferences to record these on the Work Order. Pass the ContactId context variable as contactId, the RoutableId context variable as routableId, and all 6 date/time values (preferredDate1, preferredTime1, preferredDate2, preferredTime2, preferredDate3, preferredTime3). The function will automatically find the messaging user's contact, identify the most recent Work Order linked to that contact, and update the preference fields.
After Step 1 completes, relay the confirmationMessage to the customer. The message confirms their preferences have been added to the work order and that the installer will coordinate with them to finalize scheduling.

GenAiPlugin.scope: Activates when customers provide installation scheduling preferences -- any message containing dates, times, morning, afternoon, evening, AM, or PM. Finds the contact from the messaging user, identifies the most recent Work Order, records 3 preferred installation date/time preferences, updates the Service Appointment, and automatically ends the messaging session. Cannot modify device configuration or change asset status.
